title: rhamnus saxatilis subsp. tinctoria
intro: Rhamnus saxatilis subsp. tinctoria, also known as the dyer's alder or alder buckthorn, is a subspecies of the Rhamnus saxatilis plant, commonly found in the Northern Hemisphere. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Rhamnus saxatilis subsp. tinctoria include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used to create a natural dye.
